title: SSL/TLS certificate validation completely disabled in callToCovert()
description: CorvetServiceImpl.callToCovert() creates a TrustManager[] with empty checkClientTrusted() and checkServerTrusted() implementations, accepting any certificate. This enables man-in-the-middle attacks against the CORVET service, allowing attackers to intercept VIN data, credentials, and vehicle information in transit.
beforeCode: TrustManager[] trustAllCerts = new TrustManager[] { new X509TrustManager() { public void checkClientTrusted(...) {} public void checkServerTrusted(...) {} } };

title: SSL/TLS certificate validation completely disabled in callTraxioService()
description: TraxioServiceImpl.callTraxioService() uses the same trustAllCerts pattern with empty checkClientTrusted() and checkServerTrusted(), disabling all certificate validation for calls to the Traxio third-party service. Enables MITM interception of VIN lookup data and Traxio credentials.
beforeCode: TrustManager[] trustAllCerts = new TrustManager[] { new X509TrustManager() { public void checkClientTrusted(...) {} public void checkServerTrusted(...) {} } };

title: All consumer API keys hardcoded in plain text in Consumer enum
description: The Consumer enum contains 30+ API keys in plain text (e.g. 'Q6W6WJD9duasd7qw0' for CMM, 'CB8E8CBBA1D84456A89A51281FFFD032' for F2MS, etc.). Anyone with source code access can impersonate any consumer application. API keys should be stored in a secure vault or externalized configuration, never in source code.
afterCode: // Load consumer API keys from secure vault or externalized YAML config, not from source code. Use ApplicationConfig to inject consumer registry.

title: Hardcoded API key 'qwerty78zxcvb90' sent in outbound HTTP header
description: AbstractService.getTechDocKTypeDataForKType() sends a hardcoded API key 'qwerty78zxcvb90' in the 'API-Key' header for calls to the car/ktype service. This credential is visible in source code and cannot be rotated without a code change and redeployment.

title: CORS wildcard Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * on all authenticated API responses
description: 15+ response builders across CartellServiceImpl, CorvetServiceImpl, ElectronicServiceAssistantServiceImpl, RecoverDescriptionServiceImpl, SagaiWarrantyServiceImpl, ThubServiceImpl, and VISServiceImpl set 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *'. This allows any website to make cross-origin requests to the API, potentially enabling CSRF-like attacks when combined with cookie-based or header-based auth.
afterCode: // Remove wildcard CORS. Configure allowed origins at the web server/filter level with a whitelist of trusted domains.

title: Static mutable state in StatusCode causes cross-request data leakage
description: StatusCode uses a static String field 'responseCode' that is read/written from multiple request threads simultaneously. In a Singleton service, Thread A can set responseCode='206' (timeout), and Thread B reads that stale value, returning incorrect partial-content status to an unrelated request. This is a race condition with real data integrity impact.
afterCode: private static final ThreadLocal<String> responseCode = new ThreadLocal<>();

title: New daemon Timer thread created per outbound HTTP request
description: AbstractService.setTimeout() and setTimeoutForGetRequest() each create 'new Timer(true).schedule(task, timeout)' for every outbound HTTP call. Under load, this spawns hundreds of daemon threads. Timer threads are not pooled and each one consumes OS resources. Use HttpClient's built-in RequestConfig.setConnectTimeout() / setSocketTimeout() instead.

title: VIN numbers and license plates logged at INFO level in production
description: VehicleIdentificationNumberResource.getVins() logs the full VIN number, license plate number, idToCheck, country, and consumer name at INFO level in multiple logger.info() calls (lines ~175-270). VIN and license plate numbers are personally identifiable information (PII) under GDPR. This data will persist in log aggregators and could be accessed by anyone with log access.
beforeCode: logger.info("Calling VIN Service (/vins) for Vin Search with ... vin = {}, vis = {}, licensePlateNumber = {}, idToCheck = {} ", ... vinNumber, visNumber, licencePlateNo, idToCheck);
afterCode: logger.info("Calling VIN Service (/vins) for Vin Search with consumerApp={}, Source={}, country={}", consumerName, parameter.getSource(), parameter.getCountry()); logger.debug("VIN search details: vin={}", vinNumber);

title: No rate limiting on VIN lookup endpoint enables enumeration
description: The GET /vins endpoint accepts a VIN number and returns detailed vehicle information. There is no rate limiting, throttling, or lockout mechanism. An attacker can enumerate all 17-character VINs or brute-force license plate lookups to harvest vehicle data at scale. The API-Key check alone is insufficient as keys are hardcoded and shared.

title: Null response returned to caller when CORVET call fails
description: CorvetServiceImpl.getCorvetResponse() catches IOException, NullPointerException, VinWarrantyException and VehicleIdentificationNumberBusinessException, logs the error, but returns null. The calling code in RecoverDescriptionServiceImpl/ElectronicServiceAssistantServiceImpl does not consistently check for null, leading to NullPointerExceptions that return raw 500 errors to the API client.

title: Swallowed exception in ActiveContractResource returns null response
description: ActiveContractResource.getActiveContracts() catches VehicleIdentificationNumberBusinessException at the end of the method, logs the error, but falls through to 'return response' where response may still be null. This causes a NullPointerException or empty 200 response instead of a proper error response to the client.

title: No audit trail for database write operations on vehicle data
description: AbstractService.insertVinData(), updateVinData(), insertWarranty(), and updateWarranty() perform database writes without recording which consumer application, API key, or request triggered the change. There is no audit log for who accessed or modified vehicle data, making incident investigation and compliance auditing impossible.
afterCode:
logger.info("AUDIT: insertVinData vin={} source={} country={} consumer={}", vinNumber, source, country, MDC.get("CONSUMER_NAME"));
vinRepository.insertVehicleIdentificationNumberInfoInCin(vin);

title: No structured request logging with correlation ID propagated to downstream calls
description: VehicleIdentificationNumberResource.getVins() sets MDC REQUEST_ID at the start, but downstream services (CorvetServiceImpl, SagaiWarrantyServiceImpl, ThubServiceImpl, etc.) do not propagate this correlation ID in their outbound HTTP headers. When a downstream call fails, there is no way to correlate the upstream request with the downstream failure across service boundaries.
afterCode:
MDC.put("REQUEST_ID", correlationId);
// Propagate correlationId to all downstream HTTP calls via X-Correlation-ID header

title: HealthResource.getOVHealth() reads Excel file from filesystem on every request
description: getOVHealth() opens a FileInputStream to an Excel spreadsheet (appConfig.getVinSheet()) and parses it with XSSFWorkbook on every health check invocation. This is a synchronous blocking I/O operation that loads the entire file into memory. Under concurrent health check calls, this can exhaust heap memory and block threads. The XSSFWorkbook is also never explicitly closed in the success path (only via try-with-resources on the InputStream).
beforeCode:
try (FileInputStream fis = new FileInputStream(appConfig.getVinSheet())) {
    XSSFWorkbook workbook = new XSSFWorkbook(fis);
afterCode: try (FileInputStream fis = new FileInputStream(appConfig.getVinSheet()); XSSFWorkbook workbook = new XSSFWorkbook(fis)) {

title: No application-level health check endpoint for infrastructure monitoring
description: The /health/* endpoints (ovHealth, sagaiWarrantyHealth, etc.) are functional integration tests that call external services. They are not suitable for Kubernetes/load-balancer health probes because they are slow, have side effects (DB transactions), and can false-fail when an upstream is temporarily down. The application lacks a lightweight /health or /ready endpoint that returns app status without external dependencies.
